title: [FEATURE] Sprite Animation Spritesheet Layout Definitions
author: Mr-Fishy
body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe:**
When using a spritesheet, it is not promised that the sprites will fill a full grid (i.e. 8 sprites in a 3x3 grid), neither is it promised that the sprites will always be in the top-left corner of the spritesheet. So an offset to the starting location of the grid and the total width / height of the grid needs to be known.

**Describe the solution you'd like:**
1. Add a spritesheet starting x/y offset in pixels, and the pixel width and height of the grid to the sprite component so that it can play an animation from a spritesheet with more than one animation on it.

2. Add the total number of sprites in the animation so that the grid does not have to be perfectly filled.

**Additional context:**
A continuation of #1706 .

title: RmlUI needs to track all asset dependencies
author: jankrassnigg
body:
When exporting the RTS sample the UI images are excluded, because PNG files are by default excluded. Since the RmlUI asset doesn't pull it in as a proper dependency, this doesn't automatically work and instead the file has to be included manually at the moment.

This should be made to work by detecting all transitive runtime dependencies.

title: [BUG] Broken scene does not show up in Curator when required plugin is missing
author: jankrassnigg
body:
* Make sure the SampleGamePlugin is NOT compiled in Visual Studio
* Open the Sample Game Project.
* Transform the main scene
* The log will show an error (missing component type), but nothing shows up in the Asset Curator panel

title: [BUG] RTS sample is broken due to RML UI
author: jankrassnigg
body:
When you try to place a ship in the RTS sample, it crashes in `RtsEditLevelMode::OnProcessInput()` in the line 

```cpp
ezUInt16 uiTeam = static_cast<ezUInt16>(m_pBlackboard->GetEntryValue(s_sTeam).Get<int>());
```

That's because the "Team" value is registered as an integer and thus attempted to be returned as an integer here, but RML UI overwrites it with a "string" type value.

```cpp
bool BlackboardVariableDefinition::Set(void* pPtr, const Rml::Variant& variant)
```

synchronizes stuff from RML UI to C++, and the incoming Variant is of type "String". Looking at "game-ui.rml", the values are currently indeed set up as strings:

```css
<select id="team" data-value="Team">
  <option value="0">Red</option>
  <option value="1">Green</option>
  <option value="2">Blue</option>
  <option value="3">Yellow</option>
</select>
```

But when you remove the " and just use the number values directly, it doesn't change what happens at runtime. The values are still passed as string from RML UI to the blackboards, and still overwritten.

I think if the value already exists in the blackboard, but with a differing type, that should be an assertion. It shouldn't silently change the type, as this is most likely an error.

Also it isn't clear what need so be done, such that RML UI also treats the value as an integer, rather than a string.

title: Default texture samplers should be modifyable
author: jankrassnigg
body:
Currently when a texture is set up, it gets a sampler state and then that's it, the filtering options can't be changed anymore.

But for the "default" samplers (default quality, low quality, high quality), it should be possible to just redirect to them, so that changing them after the texture is loaded is still possible.

title: [BUG] Plugin Enable Reload results in AngelScript ASSERT
author: AlexandrKedrov
body:
**Bug description:**
If "Enable reload" is activated for RML UI plugin it results in the `ezAngelScriptUtils::RegisterTypeProperties` assert with an error `asALREADY_REGISTERED`

**How to Reproduce:**
A clear description what steps to take to reproduce the issue.
1. Open Testing Chambers
1. Tick checkbox "Enable Reload" in Plugin selection for RML UI
1. Reload Editor

**Initial investigation**
During startup of the EditorEngineProcess, two DLLs are loaded:

* ezRmlUiPlugin.loaded
* ezRmlUiPlugin.dll, as a dependency of ezEnginePluginRmlUi.loaded

On Windows, these appear to be treated as two different libraries. As a result, the plugin initialization code is executed twice.

This leads to two calls to `ezRTTI::RegisterType()`, causing the same type to be added to `pData->m_AllTypes` twice:
```cpp
void ezRTTI::RegisterType()
{
  m_uiTypeNameHash = ezHashingUtils::StringHash(m_sTypeName);

  auto pData = GetTypeData();
  EZ_LOCK(pData->m_Mutex);
  pData->m_TypeNameHashToType.Insert(m_uiTypeNameHash, this);

  m_uiTypeIndex = pData->m_AllTypes.GetCount();
  pData->m_AllTypes.PushBack(this);   // << it is pushed here
}
```

`pData->m_AllTypes` is later used by the AngelScript plugin to register reflected types. Since the same type appears in this list twice, AngelScript attempts to register it twice as well, which causes the `asALREADY_REGISTERED` assertion.

I am not sure whether this is the right direction, and it would likely require a larger change, but maybe dynamic plugin dependencies could be handled more explicitly at runtime instead of relying on the default dynamic linker behavior. This might be closer to how plugin loading is already handled during the EditorFramework / EngineProcess startup.

title: [BUG] "Revert to Default" not working anymore for prefab params
author: C-Core
body:
**Bug description:**
The "Revert to Default" option does not work anymore for prefab parameters. The option is always disabled and the parameter is never highlighted in bold when overwritten.

**How to Reproduce:**
* Open a scene in the testing chambers project
* Place the "Barrel2" prefab somewhere
* Change the "Material" parameter to something else
=> the parameter is not highlighted and the "Revert to Default" option is still disabled

**Dev-branch commit hash:**
96b3b63900b4e6d285fec64e48cca4847476e386

title: [BUG]character controller: inconsistent velocity near a tilted wall, tilted ground.
author: veksha
body:
**Bug description:**

Issue was not present before this merge: https://github.com/ezEngine/ezEngine/pull/1637
by @AlexandrKedrov 

When player is moving up on a tilted surface near a tilted wall some strange force will add up to it's velocity even if player released move key already.
I assume it is Jolt stair stepping code tries to move player up a step.

so when you have a lot of objects lying around (stones) on an uneven ground and you walk near them, sometimes player speed will increase and i think it is because of this issue with stepping.

**How to Reproduce:**

[test.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/28010292/test.zip)

1. import test.ezScene to Testing Chambers
2. run the scene and go along the wall (pointing camera between moving direction and a wall).

**Videos:**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bb4e9800-2dba-46ef-83fd-11fc2e810b62

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/41677f68-8f47-46b8-99a7-db7ffb4a68f1

title: [BUG]Issue with copy/pasting all component properties
author: veksha
body:
**Bug description:**
When copy\pasting all component properties (for example, "Simple Animation" component) Some properties are not pasted (enums)

**How to Reproduce:**
1. Add Simple Animation component and change AnimationMode to "Once".
2. Right click on component header and select "Copy Value".
3. Now choose "Revert to default" from the same menu and then "Paste Value"
4. AnimationMode should be set to "Once", but it is left unchanged.

**Workaround:**
in `PropertyBaseWidget.cpp` comment out line 444:

```cpp
if (!val.CanConvertTo(pTargetProp->GetSpecificType()->GetVariantType()) && pTargetProp->GetSpecificType()->GetTypeName() != ezRTTI::FindTypeByName(content.m_Type)->GetTypeName())
                  continue;
```

title: [BUG] Undo prefab add crashes Editor
author: C-Core
body: **Bug description:** When undoing the creation of a prefab WITH exposed parameters the Editor crashes. Doesn't happen for prefabs without exposed parameter **How to Reproduce:** * Open TestingChambers project * Open any scene * Drag **Barrel2** prefab into the scene * Undo * -> Assert, then nullptr access `Expression: "uiIndex < GetCount()" Function: "ezArrayPtr const >::operator []" File: "D:\Svartvarg\_ezEngine\Code\Engine\Foundation/Types/ArrayPtr.h" Line: 226 Message: "Cannot access element 0, the array only holds 0 elements."` **Callstack:** **Dev-branch commit hash:** 3e4c2579b3564baf174ecd9d0e87468ff3b76aeb

title: [BUG]Particle Fog Blending Issue
author: Lt-droid
body: **Bug description:** When using a particle with fog blending ,in a specific case .Indeed it doesn't color accurately. This is particularly visible at short range , between the viewer and the particle system. **How to Reproduce:** In "testing chamber", 1. cover the entire level with a wide greybox.(duplicate the floor for instance) 2. create a fog component with white color (255,255,255 )and height falloff at 1000 3. in the asset explorer, search for "**Explosion**" 4. save as "**Explosion2**" 5. in this particle system remove everything except the "**smoke**" system 6. also remove "**wind**" and "**move**" behaviors 7. save it 8. place it over the grey floor outside 9. place a cube mesh next to it with a black colored material assigned 10. Drag "**Explosion2**" out of tab group 11. First set "**Explosion2**" blending to "**Opaque**" and lighting to "**Fullbright**" in the "Renderers" section. 12. Execute the "**Main**" scene and be sure to be near the particle and box created. 13. Now Enable/disable the white fog 14. Do the same with every "**Explosion2**" blending and lit options **Screenshots:** Here are more self explanatory pictures: fog ON: fog OFF: **Dev-branch commit hash:** 2b68e48e

title: Live GPU resources at shutdown
author: antonyjones67
body:
Hey guys I noticed that, the debug-layer output at shutdown showed a long list of live GPU resource entries (Bistro mesh buffers, RTs, fallback resources, etc.) — the engine still owns them when ezGALDevice::Shutdown() runs.

Take a look at the PDF.

[IssueRenderTeardown.pdf](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27309825/IssueRenderTeardown.pdf)

title: Materials with AssetFilterTags can't be selected as base material
author: C-Core
body:
**Bug description:**
When a material has an Asset Filter Tag set it does not show up when you try to select it as base material for another material.

**How to Reproduce:**
* Create a particle material in VSE and set the Asset Filter Tag to "QuadParticle"
* Create another material and try to pick the first material as base to e.g. overwrite some settings. It doesn't show up in the asset browser to pick as base.

title: [FEATURE] copy/paste component or its values
author: veksha
body: **Describe the solution you'd like:** - [ ] copy/paste component from one object to another. - [ ] copy/paste all property values to component on a different object. Pasting the values could be also allowed to **any** component _type_ (as long as property names are matching) For example, from **default** character controller to **custom** character controller (which has almost the same properties) **Additional context:** How it looks in Unity:

title: [BUG]Move/scale/rotate/drag GIZMO mouse issue
author: Lt-droid
body:
**Bug description:**
When trying transforming any objects,
Some problems with the mouse clicking over the corresponding AXIS.
Furthermore,It doesn't highlight properly.
This seems to be very obvious at **higher fov**.
This issue is **Barely noticable** in 90°.

**How to Reproduce:**
A clear description what steps to take to reproduce the issue.

1. In preferences, set the fov to 150°.
2. In any scene , select any objects
3. Then Click "Translate" for instance.
4. Now cycle your mouse over the displayed AXIS.
5. You will notice some highlighting issues.
6. Do the same for Rotate/scale/drag to position

title: [BUG] Editor asserts if auto-save happens while user is dragging something
author: jankrassnigg
body: The auto-save feature needs to wait for the current transaction to finish, before it can save something, otherwise there is an error.

title: [BUG] ImGui CVars is not showing all vars
author: veksha
body: **Bug description:** Some CVars are hidden in ImGui's CVars table. For example, boolean cvar "ProcGen.VisTiles" has children like "ProcGen.VisTiles.OutputFilter", etc.... so in ImGui's table it will become parent **node** for its children. It can be accessed through Commands (console) window, though. **Screenshots:**
